source_pr.title: *: support password reuse policy
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #38937

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?
 the feature is the same as MySQL's .

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
 support password reuse policy like MySQL . 
```

symptoms: rootTK.MustExec(`update mysql.password_history set Password_timestamp = date_sub(Password_timestamp,interval '3 0:0:1' DAY_SECOND) where user = 'testReuse' order by Password_timestamp asc limit 1`) (executor/simple_test.go)
failure_signature: rootTK.MustExec(`update mysql.password_history set Password_timestamp = date_sub(Password_timestamp,interval '3 0:0:1' DAY_SECOND) where user = 'testReuse' order by Password_timestamp asc limit 1`) (executor/simple_test.go)
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: rootTK.MustExec(`update mysql.password_history set Password_timestamp = date_sub(Password_timestamp,interval '3 0:0:1' DAY_SECOND) where user = 'testReuse' order by Password_timestamp asc limit 1`) (executor/simple_test.go)

files: Makefile, errno/errcode.go, errno/errname.go, errors.toml, executor/errors.go, executor/grant_test.go, executor/infoschema_cluster_table_test.go, executor/show.go, executor/showtest/show_test.go, executor/simple.go, executor/simple_test.go, executor/simpletest/simple_test.go, parser/ast/misc.go, parser/misc.go, parser/mysql/const.go, parser/parser.go, parser/parser.y, server/http_handler_serial_test.go, session/bootstrap.go, session/bootstrap_test.go, sessionctx/variable/sysvar.go, sessionctx/variable/tidb_vars.go
